ecap-skills.author: allenz-srp
ecap-skills.message:
chore(devcontainer): wire @zooclaw/web-fetch into openclaw config (#192)

Adds the zooclaw-web-fetch plugin entry under plugins.entries and sets
tools.web.fetch.provider="zooclaw" so the devcontainer dispatches
web_fetch through the new plugin once openclaw-docker bakes it into
the image (ECA-624 W2 work).

The plugin is configured with no inline config â€” proxyUrl (from
ECAP_PROXY_BASE_URL) and the bearer token (from USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN)
are both injected by initializeCommand.sh's fastclaw deployment.env
fetch.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
ecap-skills.pr_body:
## Summary

- Wires `@zooclaw/web-fetch` (zooclaw-extras#28) into the devcontainer's `openclaw.json.tmpl` so `web_fetch` tool calls dispatch through the new ZooClaw fetch proxy once openclaw-docker bakes the plugin into the image (ECA-624 W2).
- Adds two pieces:
  - `tools.web.fetch.provider="zooclaw"` â€” selects the new provider when present
  - `plugins.entries.zooclaw-web-fetch: { enabled: true }` â€” enables the plugin (no inline config; `proxyUrl` and the bearer token come from `ECAP_PROXY_BASE_URL` / `USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN` env, which `initializeCommand.sh` already pulls from fastclaw deployment.env)

## Why this is safe to land before openclaw-docker bake

OpenClaw's `web_fetch` dispatch only considers bundled `webFetchProviders`; user-installed plugins of that contract are loaded but never selected. So with the current image:
- `tools.web.fetch.provider="zooclaw"` is logged as `WEB_FETCH_PROVIDER_INVALID_AUTODETECT` and the runtime falls back to its built-in Readability pipeline â€” same behavior as before.
- Once openclaw-docker bakes `@zooclaw/web-fetch` (ECA-624 W2), this config becomes active immediately, no further devcontainer change needed.

## Related work

- **zooclaw-extras PR #28** â€” `@zooclaw/web-fetch` plugin (Bearer auth, env-fed config)
- **ecap-proxy-service PR #40** â€” `/fetch/proxy` Firecrawl-backed route
- **Linear ECA-624** â€” parent ticket

## Test plan

- [x] `initializeCommand.sh` re-renders `openclaw.json` from the new template; `jq .plugins.entries` shows `zooclaw-web-fetch`, `jq .tools.web.fetch.provider` returns `"zooclaw"`.
- [x] Smoke test: with the plugin manually copied into the image's bundled extensions dir (one-off hack), gateway dispatch reaches the plugin's HTTP layer and emits the expected `target=â€¦/fetch/proxy` log line. End-to-end via `host.docker.internal:8000` is blocked by openclaw's SSRF guard â€” a dev-env artifact (private IP); staging/prod URLs unaffected.
- [ ] After openclaw-docker bakes the plugin: rebuild devcontainer, send a `web_fetch` from chat, verify both gateway and ecap-proxy-service logs show the call.

ecap-workspace.author: sam-srp
ecap-workspace.message:
fix(web): surface componentStack to Sentry from chat ErrorBoundary (#1572)

## Summary
- `ChatErrorBoundary` (`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/GenClawClient.tsx`)
auto-recovers render crashes by remounting children with a fresh `key`.
It currently only `logger.warn`s, so any error caught here â€” including
the recurring `Maximum update depth exceeded` (React #185) â€” never
reaches Sentry. We've been blind to which component is looping.
- Add a throttled `captureChatError('render_recovery', ...)` call that
includes `componentStack` and a `recoveryCount`. The 2s/per-message
throttle prevents a render loop (50+ throws/sec) from flooding the local
breadcrumb buffer and crowding out the actual cause.
- Improve the global `ErrorBoundary`
(`web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`): add `tags.boundary = 'global'`
and mirror `componentStack` into `extra` so it's visible in Sentry's
issue list view, not just inside the React context tab.

## Why
Sentry issue ECAP-WEBSITE-MA (`Maximum update depth exceeded`) keeps
firing on `/chat` with no React component stack â€” debugging is stuck on
minified call stacks. With this change, the next occurrence will surface
the exact React component tree path (e.g. `TooltipContent â†’
DismissableLayer â†’ Popper`), letting us pinpoint the looping component
without enabling production source maps.

No behavior change for users â€” auto-recovery flow in `ChatErrorBoundary`
is preserved.

## Test plan
- [ ] `cd web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] `cd web && pnpm lint` passes (already ran via pre-commit)
- [ ] In dev, throw a synthetic error inside the chat thread and
confirm: (a) UI auto-recovers as before, (b) a `monitor:chat` +
`chat_feature:render_recovery` event lands in Sentry with
`componentStack` populated
- [ ] Trigger an error outside the chat thread (e.g. SideNav) and
confirm the global boundary captures with `boundary:global` tag and
`extra.componentStack`

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
ecap-workspace.pr_title: fix(web): surface componentStack to Sentry from chat ErrorBoundary
ecap-workspace.pr_body:
## Summary
- `ChatErrorBoundary` (`web/src/app/[locale]/chat/GenClawClient.tsx`) auto-recovers render crashes by remounting children with a fresh `key`. It currently only `logger.warn`s, so any error caught here â€” including the recurring `Maximum update depth exceeded` (React #185) â€” never reaches Sentry. We've been blind to which component is looping.
- Add a throttled `captureChatError('render_recovery', ...)` call that includes `componentStack` and a `recoveryCount`. The 2s/per-message throttle prevents a render loop (50+ throws/sec) from flooding the local breadcrumb buffer and crowding out the actual cause.
- Improve the global `ErrorBoundary` (`web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`): add `tags.boundary = 'global'` and mirror `componentStack` into `extra` so it's visible in Sentry's issue list view, not just inside the React context tab.

## Why
Sentry issue ECAP-WEBSITE-MA (`Maximum update depth exceeded`) keeps firing on `/chat` with no React component stack â€” debugging is stuck on minified call stacks. With this change, the next occurrence will surface the exact React component tree path (e.g. `TooltipContent â†’ DismissableLayer â†’ Popper`), letting us pinpoint the looping component without enabling production source maps.

No behavior change for users â€” auto-recovery flow in `ChatErrorBoundary` is preserved.

## Test plan
- [ ] `cd web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] `cd web && pnpm lint` passes (already ran via pre-commit)
- [ ] In dev, throw a synthetic error inside the chat thread and confirm: (a) UI auto-recovers as before, (b) a `monitor:chat` + `chat_feature:render_recovery` event lands in Sentry with `componentStack` populated
- [ ] Trigger an error outside the chat thread (e.g. SideNav) and confirm the global boundary captures with `boundary:global` tag and `extra.componentStack`

ecap-workspace.author: kaka-srp
ecap-workspace.message:
feat(agents): agent-studio uses 1/10 sonnet, hide from picker (#1573)

## Summary

Two threads, both centered on Linear
[ECA-636](https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-636)'s "Agent Studio gets
a 1/10-priced Sonnet variant":

1. **Wire `agent-studio-sonnet-4-6` into the `agent_studio` builtin
agent at hire time.** `apply_agents_list` now reads `meta["model"]` from
the catalog row and writes `{"primary":
"openai/agent-studio-sonnet-4-6"}` into the per-agent entry. Catalog row
in code (`LEGACY_OFFICIAL_AGENT_MAP`) gains the field; the staging Mongo
row was already patched manually before this PR (prod still owes the
same `PATCH /openclaw/internal/agent-catalog/agent_studio`).
2. **Consolidate the user-facing model-id filter on the backend.** New
`INTERNAL_MODEL_IDS` (exact) + `INTERNAL_MODEL_SUBSTRINGS`
(case-insensitive) constants in `plan_models.py` plus
`is_internal_model` / `filter_internal_models` helpers. Both
`resolve_models_by_type` and `resolve_models_for_plan` apply the filter,
so the `/openclaw/settings` `available_models` payload and the
`update_model` validation see the same hidden list. Two duplicate
frontend `embedding` filters (`ModelSection.tsx`,
`AgentModelSection.tsx`) deleted now that the backend is canonical.

End-to-end verified on staging: hired `agent_studio` for a test account,
confirmed the model id flowed all the way to
`/home/node/.openclaw/openclaw.json` on the bot pod, observed the
OpenClaw runtime fallback chain via `[model-fallback/decision]` logs,
then confirmed the discount price after LiteLLM team-group access was
added by the platform team.

## Test plan

- [x] Backend unit tests: `test_plan_models.py` (10 new),
`test_agent_catalog.py` (1 new),
`test_openclaw_agents.py::TestApplyAgentsList` (4 new) â€” all green
- [x] Frontend unit tests: `ModelSection.unit.spec.tsx`,
`AgentModelSection.unit.spec.tsx` â€” all green
- [x] ruff / pyright / ESLint clean on touched files
- [x] Manual E2E on staging: hire `agent_studio`, verify
`agents.list[].model.primary` in bot's `openclaw.json`, verify upstream
model in chat
- [ ] CI green (`code-quality / lint-and-test` + `python-code-quality /
build-and-test`)

## Rollout note

Production deploy still requires a one-time `agent_catalog` row patch:
\`\`\`bash
curl -X PATCH
"\$CLAW_INTERFACE_URL/openclaw/internal/agent-catalog/agent_studio" \\
  -H 'content-type: application/json' -H "\$INTERNAL_AUTH_HEADER" \\
  -d '{"model": "openai/agent-studio-sonnet-4-6"}'
\`\`\`
Code-only deploy without this patch is a no-op for users on prod (their
DB row still has no \`model\` field).
ecap-workspace.pr_title: feat(agents): agent-studio uses 1/10 sonnet, hide from picker
ecap-workspace.pr_body:
## Summary

Two threads, both centered on Linear [ECA-636](https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-636)'s "Agent Studio gets a 1/10-priced Sonnet variant":

1. **Wire `agent-studio-sonnet-4-6` into the `agent_studio` builtin agent at hire time.** `apply_agents_list` now reads `meta["model"]` from the catalog row and writes `{"primary": "openai/agent-studio-sonnet-4-6"}` into the per-agent entry. Catalog row in code (`LEGACY_OFFICIAL_AGENT_MAP`) gains the field; the staging Mongo row was already patched manually before this PR (prod still owes the same `PATCH /openclaw/internal/agent-catalog/agent_studio`).
2. **Consolidate the user-facing model-id filter on the backend.** New `INTERNAL_MODEL_IDS` (exact) + `INTERNAL_MODEL_SUBSTRINGS` (case-insensitive) constants in `plan_models.py` plus `is_internal_model` / `filter_internal_models` helpers. Both `resolve_models_by_type` and `resolve_models_for_plan` apply the filter, so the `/openclaw/settings` `available_models` payload and the `update_model` validation see the same hidden list. Two duplicate frontend `embedding` filters (`ModelSection.tsx`, `AgentModelSection.tsx`) deleted now that the backend is canonical.

End-to-end verified on staging: hired `agent_studio` for a test account, confirmed the model id flowed all the way to `/home/node/.openclaw/openclaw.json` on the bot pod, observed the OpenClaw runtime fallback chain via `[model-fallback/decision]` logs, then confirmed the discount price after LiteLLM team-group access was added by the platform team.

## Test plan

- [x] Backend unit tests: `test_plan_models.py` (10 new), `test_agent_catalog.py` (1 new), `test_openclaw_agents.py::TestApplyAgentsList` (4 new) â€” all green
- [x] Frontend unit tests: `ModelSection.unit.spec.tsx`, `AgentModelSection.unit.spec.tsx` â€” all green
- [x] ruff / pyright / ESLint clean on touched files
- [x] Manual E2E on staging: hire `agent_studio`, verify `agents.list[].model.primary` in bot's `openclaw.json`, verify upstream model in chat
- [ ] CI green (`code-quality / lint-and-test` + `python-code-quality / build-and-test`)

## Rollout note

Production deploy still requires a one-time `agent_catalog` row patch:
\`\`\`bash
curl -X PATCH "\$CLAW_INTERFACE_URL/openclaw/internal/agent-catalog/agent_studio" \\
  -H 'content-type: application/json' -H "\$INTERNAL_AUTH_HEADER" \\
  -d '{"model": "openai/agent-studio-sonnet-4-6"}'
\`\`\`
Code-only deploy without this patch is a no-op for users on prod (their DB row still has no \`model\` field).
